data.body:
PASS 1 review (criterion T11 — Data-migration / backfill safety) — no findings.

T11 is an OVERLAY that applies only when a plan changes a schema / persisted format or backfills data; this plan does neither. Verified against the code, not just the plan text.

Affirmations (T11 correctly not-applicable):
- All optimizations are runtime/in-memory or provider-side: S1 = Anthropic ephemeral prompt/system-prompt caching (anthropic_cache_instructions/tool_definitions, runner.py:421-435); S3 = parallelize/warm-then-fan-out the container fan-out (concurrency, no persisted state); S2 = "relocating per-run ticket data out of the system prompt" is a prompt-ASSEMBLY refactor (no on-disk format); S4/S5 = merge G3+G4 / bin-pack children (call structuring); S6 = add eval_specs fixtures (test data, not a runtime persisted format). None introduce or alter a persisted schema.
- The only on-disk artifact is the per-ticket checkpoint cache (.rebar/cache/plan-review/<ticket_id>/), and it carries NO migration obligation: it is git-ignored and disposable (_checkpoint_dir, sizing.py:246-251); content-keyed on the ticket MATERIAL fingerprint + criterion ids + model + tier so any material change cold-misses and ignores the stale entry (_checkpoint_key, sizing.py:254-259); written atomically (tmp + rename, save_checkpoint sizing.py:287-299); and best-effort on both read and write (any error ⇒ recompute / proceed, load_checkpoint/save_checkpoint). There is no backfill, no blocking DDL on a large table, no dual-write window, and no data-loss surface to guard.

No expand-contract, batching/throttling, resumability, dual-write, or rollback obligations attach. T11 PASSES as not-applicable.
